name: Europium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Europium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Europium oxide layer removal

wavelength.description: Green wavelength for optimal Europium absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning

fluenceThreshold.description: Threshold fluence for Europium oxide removal

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse width for controlled ablation

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for complete coverage

power.description: Typical average power for pulsed laser cleaning of soft metals like Europium to avoid melting (mp 826°C); conservative range due to low thermal conductivity (~14 W/m·K); recommended starting low to test. Source: General manufacturer specs (e.g., Coherent cleaning systems for metals, 10-200 W range); limited specific Europium data, adapted from Al cleaning studies (Opt. Lasers Eng., 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides on reactive Europium), faster for light; optimal balances ablation and heat dissipation. Source: Industry standards (e.g., Laser Cleaning Handbook by Trumpf, 100-10,000 mm/s); case studies on rare earth alloys (J. Laser Appl., 2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning with minimal HAZ on low-melting Europium; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduce thermal effects but increase cost; ns avoids excessive heat accumulation. Source: Laser physics research (e.g., Appl. Surf. Sci., 2019 on metal ablation thresholds); ns typical for 532 nm cleaning.

repetition_rate.description: Balances cleaning speed and heat buildup; higher rates accelerate process but risk thermal damage to Europium's soft structure; moderate rate recommended for uniform ablation. Source: Academic literature (e.g., Opt. Express, 2017 on pulsed laser cleaning of metals; trade-offs noted in high-rep-rate studies).

energy_density.description: Fluence for contaminant ablation without exceeding Europium damage threshold (~1.5-3 J/cm² estimated from similar soft metals); low values due to high reflectivity at 532 nm and low mp; safe cleaning below threshold. Source: Ablation threshold studies (e.g., J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys., 2021 on rare earth metals; conservative estimate for Europium).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise surface preparation; smaller for fine details on Europium components, larger for broad areas; 200 μm balances resolution and efficiency. Source: Equipment specifications (e.g., IPG Photonics scanners, 50-1000 μm); adapted for industrial cleaning of reactive metals.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass for thorough removal of contaminants (e.g., oxides) on Europium without single-pass overheating; single pass for light cleaning, 2-3 typical for industrial prep. Source: Industry practice (e.g., case studies in Surf. Coat. Technol., 2019 on metal surface cleaning); conservative for soft substrates.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage and overlap for complete cleaning; higher for heavy contamination on irregular Europium surfaces, lower for speed; 70% optimal for even energy distribution. Source: Process optimization studies (e.g., Lasers Manuf. Mater. Process., 2022; standard 50-80% in laser scanning protocols).
